task_completion.description: Start a synthetic Cyton board session in the OpenBCI GUI
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI is open and actively running a session, indicated by flowing EEG data in the Time Series widget and system status showing a 'Synthetic' Cyton board connection.
The Hardware Settings or Channel Settings panel is expanded/visible in the OpenBCI GUI, displaying the PGA Gain controls for the individual channels.

task_completion.description: Set the PGA Gain for Channels 1 and 2 to 12x
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenBCI GUI hardware settings, the gain dropdown or indicator for Channel 1 and Channel 2 is explicitly set to '12x'.

task_completion.description: Set the PGA Gain for Channels 3 through 6 to 24x
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenBCI GUI hardware settings, the gain dropdown or indicator for Channels 3, 4, 5, and 6 is explicitly set to '24x'.

task_completion.description: Set the PGA Gain for Channels 7 and 8 to 8x
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenBCI GUI hardware settings, the gain dropdown or indicator for Channel 7 and Channel 8 is explicitly set to '8x'.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the configuration to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or image viewer shows a screenshot file named 'gain_config.png' located in '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/').

task_completion.description: Create a text report listing the settings at the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displays the contents of 'gain_report.txt' in '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/' (or '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/'), and the text clearly lists the configured gain settings for all 8 channels.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI to configure settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the OpenBCI GUI menus and dropdowns, rather than just using terminal commands or editing configuration files directly.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is a genuine capture of the configured GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The image saved as 'gain_config.png' should depict the actual OpenBCI GUI with the correct hardware settings visible, not a generic image or an empty file.

integrity.description: The text report was genuinely created and saved
integrity.visual_evidence: The text report should be visible in a standard text editor (like nano, gedit, or VS Code) or terminal output (like cat), showing it was actually written to the correct directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected screenshot path: /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/gain_config.png. Expected report path: /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/gain_report.txt. Required PGA Gain settings: Channels 1 & 2 = 12x, Channels 3, 4, 5, & 6 = 24x, Channels 7 & 8 = 8x.
